name: Nyahkur

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Nyahkur (nyah1250).

notes: Austroasiatic (Monic) language of central Thailand, a close relative of Mon, written with the Thai script. The Thai abugida ties consonant symbols to an inherent vowel and layers pre-, post-, above- and below-base vowel signs plus consonant-class and tone marking, so the surface reading of a spelling depends on syllable structure and cannot be read off letter by letter. A segmental IPA rendering therefore requires a per-word pronunciation lexicon the engine does not bundle; grapheme->IPA coverage is lexicon-dependent and effectively zero without that stratum, and no alphabetic letter->IPA table is asserted. Nyahkur retains a register/vowel-quality contrast rather than lexical tone, and any such register distinction is left unencoded.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Nyahkur (nyah1250)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
